revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:石川護国神社]]
{{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:06 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 石川護国神社.jpg | image=[[File:石川護国神社.jpg|250px|拝殿]] | name=石川護国神社 | location=石川県金沢市石引4-18-1 | deity=石川県関連の国事殉難者 | type=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 | established=明治3rd year （1870th year ） | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 33.59 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 39 | 経度秒 = 50.17 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map国コード = JP | map = Japan Kanazawa city2#Japan Kanazawa city#Japan Ishikawa#Japan }}
'''Ishikawa Gokoku Shrine''' (石川護国神社, Ishikawa Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|qid=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa, Ishikawa|1=Kanazawa}} in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is situated next to {{ill|Kenroku-en|en|Kenroku-en|qid=Q998239|lt=Kenroku-en|1=Kenroku-en}}, in the center of the city. The shrine boasts the largest area among shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture.
The shrine's name can also be written in the old kanji form as 石川護國神社<ref name="gk257" />. The official website uses this old form as well.
== Overview == The shrine was initially established as a {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810}} on December 15, 1870 (Meiji 3) by the 14th {{ill|daimyō|ja|藩主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藩|1=Han system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q841985}} of the {{ill|Kaga Domain|en|Kaga Domain|qid=Q903448|lt=Kaga Domain|1=Kaga Domain}}, {{ill|Maeda Yoshiyasu|en|Maeda Yoshiyasu|qid=Q6729123|lt=Maeda Yoshiyasu|1=Maeda Yoshiyasu}}, to enshrine the spirits of 107 individuals from the Kaga Domain, including Mizuno Tokusaburo Hirotomo, who died in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. At that time, it was located on {{ill|Mount Utatsu|en|Mount Utatsu|qid=Q6924291|lt=Utatsuyama|1=Mount Utatsu}}. In June 1901 (Meiji 34), it became an official government-sponsored shōkonsha<ref name="gk257">『全國護國神社會五十年史』（1997th year 3月1日、全國護國神社會発行）257頁。</ref>.
Due to the small area of the precincts on Utatsuyama, which made it difficult to conduct ceremonies, the shrine was moved to its current location on April 13, 1935 (Shōwa 10), a section of the former army training ground in {{ill|Kodatsuno|ja|小立野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodatsuno|1=Kodatsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653046}}. On April 1, 1939 (Shōwa 14), it was renamed Ishikawa Gokoku Shrine by the designation of the Minister of Home Affairs<ref name="gk257" />.
After World War II, on April 1, 1949 (Shōwa 24), the shrine's name was temporarily changed to "Ishikawa Shrine," but it was restored to "Ishikawa Gokoku Shrine" on April 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27)<ref name="gk257" />.
On October 19, 1970 (Shōwa 45), to commemorate the shrine's 100th anniversary, renovations were carried out, including the refurbishment of the main worship hall's furnishings and the repair of the torii gate<ref name="gk257" />.
The Ishikawa Gokoku Shrine Support Association was established on September 2, 1983 (Shōwa 58)<ref name="gk257" />.
On October 15, 1984 (Shōwa 59), to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the shrine's relocation, the shrine office was renovated. On October 19, 1990 (Heisei 2), the 120th-anniversary commemorative project was completed, including the re-roofing of the shrine buildings<ref name="gk257" />.
=== Greater East Asia War Monument ===
On August 4, 2000 (Heisei 12), the Greater East Asia War Monument was erected along the approach to the shrine. On October 11, 2010 (Heisei 22), a ceremony was held to commemorate the completion of a sub-monument titled "Know the Truth of the Glorious Divine Destiny War." == Notes ==
